nodes.summary: Implemented a shared workspace settings system so the settings modal now controls real UI behavior across the app. The settings state was lifted to the page and threaded into the sidebar, artifact viewer, command center, and modal. Theme now updates the overall shell and panel styling, density changes spacing, font size adjusts artifact text/table rendering, line numbers can be toggled in the viewer, and auto-scroll can be disabled in the chat panel. The settings modal was also kept as a dedicated overlay component, with the About section removed earlier and the modal now reflecting the shared theme state.

affectedFiles.reason: Lifted settings state to the workspace page and passed shared settings into the sidebar, artifact viewer, and command center.
affectedFiles.notes: Also updated the mobile shell to reflect theme changes.

affectedFiles.reason: Converted modal controls to receive shared settings state and dispatch updates instead of maintaining isolated local state.
affectedFiles.notes: The modal now reflects the app theme and density state.

affectedFiles.reason: Sidebar now passes the shared settings state into the modal so theme changes and control updates can affect the whole app.
affectedFiles.notes: The settings button still opens the modal.

affectedFiles.reason: Artifact rendering now responds to shared line-number, font-size, theme, and density settings.
affectedFiles.notes: Text and sheet rendering both use the shared settings contract.

affectedFiles.reason: Chat panel now respects shared theme, density, and auto-scroll settings.
affectedFiles.notes: Auto-scroll can be toggled off from the modal.

affectedFiles.reason: Introduced a shared typed settings contract to avoid duplicating setting shapes and to keep state updates explicit.
affectedFiles.notes: Defines the default workspace settings and updater type.

nodes.summary: Adjusted the chat UI layout so the command center message list scrolls independently and the composer stays attached to the bottom without a separator line. The change was made primarily in the CommandCenter component, with a small parent layout tweak in the page to ensure the flex column can shrink correctly.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains the message list and composer layout; updated to make the message list scrollable and remove the separator above the composer.
affectedFiles.notes: Added flex sizing and overflow handling to the message list wrapper and removed the composer top border.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the chat panel container whose flex sizing must allow the internal scroll area to shrink properly.
affectedFiles.notes: Added min-h-0 to the chat panel wrapper and its sibling split panel wrapper.

nodes.summary: Implemented a Supabase-backed workspace and chat flow. The app now loads real workspaces from Supabase, allows creating a workspace through a modal opened from a plus button in the sidebar and mobile header, and loads workspace-specific chat history through a workspace chat API. The chat send path now requires a valid workspace, validates that conversations belong to the selected workspace, and falls back to creating a conversation inside that workspace when needed. No SQL migration changes were required because the existing workspaces/conversations/messages schema already supported the flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Replaced mock workspace state with Supabase-loaded workspaces, added workspace creation flow, and wired the page to the new chat loader and modal.
affectedFiles.notes: Main UI orchestrator for the feature.

affectedFiles.reason: Sidebar now shows real workspace rows, adds a plus button for creating workspaces, and handles the empty-workspace state.
affectedFiles.notes: Desktop navigation entrypoint.

affectedFiles.reason: New modal component for creating workspaces from the sidebar/mobile header CTA with validation and inline error handling.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps workspace creation UI isolated.

affectedFiles.reason: Hook now loads persisted workspace chat history from Supabase before streaming new messages and blocks sends while history is loading.
affectedFiles.notes: Client-side chat state and streaming logic.

affectedFiles.reason: New API route to list workspaces and create a new workspace with validation.
affectedFiles.notes: Server boundary for workspace CRUD.

affectedFiles.reason: New API route to return the latest conversation and persisted messages for a workspace.
affectedFiles.notes: Server boundary for workspace-specific chat loading.

affectedFiles.reason: Chat streaming route now enforces valid workspace ownership for conversations and no longer falls back to the old auto-created default workspace flow.
affectedFiles.notes: Prevents chats from escaping the selected workspace.

affectedFiles.reason: Added a listWorkspaces helper used by the new workspace listing API.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps workspace data access centralized.

affectedFiles.reason: Added workspace conversation listing helpers used by the workspace chat loader.
affectedFiles.notes: Supports selecting the active workspace conversation.

affectedFiles.reason: Added a neutral empty-state panel so the chat area still has a useful workspace-ready state when there are no messages.
affectedFiles.notes: Improves UX for newly created or empty workspaces.

affectedFiles.reason: Verified the existing schema already contained workspaces, conversations, and messages, so no migration was needed for this feature.
affectedFiles.notes: No file changes required.

nodes.summary: User wants the left workspace panel (ArtifactViewer area) to support uploading files, extract text from txt/md/pdf/docx into line-based content, persist the extracted content in Supabase, and leave a clean hook for a future AI file-reading tool. I inspected the workspace page, ArtifactViewer, chat input, workspace repository, Supabase schema/types, and existing API patterns to shape the plan.

affectedFiles.reason: This is the top-level workspace composition and controls the left ArtifactViewer panel where upload support should be integrated.
affectedFiles.notes: Contains current workspace state, sample artifacts, and panel layout.

affectedFiles.reason: This is the workspace panel component that will likely host upload/dropzone and file preview behavior.
affectedFiles.notes: Currently only renders tabs, content, and an empty state CTA.

affectedFiles.reason: This empty state is the most likely UI entry point for adding upload affordances and supported-format guidance.
affectedFiles.notes: Currently only offers a sample deploy button.

affectedFiles.reason: Contains an existing attachment button that may inform or share UX patterns for file input styling.
affectedFiles.notes: No file upload logic exists yet.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the established Supabase repository pattern to mirror for workspace file persistence.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for future repository separation.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the repository structure and validation/error-handling style to follow for new file ingestion helpers.
affectedFiles.notes: Good reference for server-side data access conventions.

affectedFiles.reason: This file will need to be extended to include any new file/document tables introduced by the migration.
affectedFiles.notes: Must stay in sync with Supabase schema.

affectedFiles.reason: Baseline migration shows current tables and trigger/RLS patterns to extend for uploaded files.
affectedFiles.notes: New migration should follow the same style.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the existing API style for workspace list/create routes, which new file routes should match.
affectedFiles.notes: Validates JSON handling and error responses.

affectedFiles.reason: Shows the pattern for workspace-scoped API routes that the file ingestion route should mirror.
affectedFiles.notes: Useful for params handling and 404 behavior.

nodes.summary: Implemented workspace file upload and text extraction flow. The workspace panel now exposes a real upload button and empty-state CTA, uploads txt/md/pdf/docx through a workspace-scoped API route, extracts normalized line-based text on the server, persists uploaded file metadata and extracted lines in Supabase, and keeps a repository hook ready for future AI file-reading tools.

affectedFiles.reason: Adds the new Supabase table and related indexes/triggers needed to persist uploaded files and extracted lines.
affectedFiles.notes: Includes RLS enablement and updated_at trigger.

affectedFiles.reason: Extends the generated-style database contract with workspace file row, insert, and relationship types.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps client/server type usage explicit.

affectedFiles.reason: Centralizes file-type allowlisting, size limits, and accept-string generation for upload validation.
affectedFiles.notes: Shared by client and server code.

affectedFiles.reason: Implements server-side extraction and normalization for txt, md, pdf, and docx files into line arrays.
affectedFiles.notes: Uses built-in parsing logic rather than external packages.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides Supabase persistence and retrieval helpers for uploaded workspace files, plus a reusable line-range helper for future AI reads.
affectedFiles.notes: Server-side data access only.

affectedFiles.reason: Wraps the workspace file API for the client-side page orchestration layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Handles fetch, upload, and response validation.

affectedFiles.reason: Converts persisted file rows into ArtifactViewer artifacts so uploaded files render with the existing line-based viewer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps UI mapping separate from data access.

affectedFiles.reason: Exposes workspace-scoped GET and POST endpoints for listing and uploading extracted files.
affectedFiles.notes: Validates workspace existence and multipart uploads.

affectedFiles.reason: Adds the workspace file header upload control to the left panel while keeping the viewer composition-focused.
affectedFiles.notes: Now accepts fileCount and onUploadFiles props.

affectedFiles.reason: Reworks the empty state into a file upload call to action with the supported document formats.
affectedFiles.notes: Removed the old sample deploy wording.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the new top-of-panel upload entry point and file count display without bloating the viewer entry file.
affectedFiles.notes: Uses neutral styling consistent with the app.

affectedFiles.reason: Orchestrates workspace file loading, upload actions, hidden file input handling, and artifact state synchronization.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the page as the composition layer and delegates extraction/persistence to helpers.

nodes.summary: Fixed the workspace file upload failure caused by malformed Unicode reaching Supabase. The ingestion pipeline now sanitizes extracted and inserted text to remove NULs and lone surrogates before persistence, which should prevent the Postgres Unicode escape error during file uploads.

affectedFiles.reason: Added Unicode normalization and sanitization for extracted file text, line arrays, and upload metadata before persistence.
affectedFiles.notes: Removes NUL characters and lone surrogates.

affectedFiles.reason: Added defensive sanitization before the Supabase insert to prevent malformed Unicode from reaching the database.
affectedFiles.notes: Provides a second validation layer at persistence time.

nodes.summary: Switched workspace file ingestion from hand-rolled parsing to dedicated PDF/DOCX libraries. The upload path now targets @cedrugs/pdf-parse for PDFs and mammoth for DOCX, while continuing to normalize extracted text into numbered lines for the viewer and future AI file-reading tool. Added ambient declarations for the new modules and updated package.json dependencies accordingly.

affectedFiles.reason: Replaced the custom PDF/DOCX extraction heuristics with proper parser-based extraction so uploads yield readable text lines.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps sanitization and line normalization after extraction.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the new parser dependencies required for readable PDF and DOCX extraction.
affectedFiles.notes: Dependencies need to be installed into node_modules.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the mammoth parser import used by the ingestion layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides an explicit TypeScript contract for the PDF parser import used by the ingestion layer.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the module boundary typed.

nodes.summary: Updated workspace document ingestion so extracted PDF/DOCX/TXT/MD content now drops leading and trailing blank lines before storing both the numbered line array and the concatenated extracted text. This keeps the downstream contract intact while removing empty boundary lines from uploaded documents.

affectedFiles.reason: Implemented trimming of leading and trailing empty lines during workspace document ingestion and rebuilt stored extracted text from the trimmed lines.
affectedFiles.notes: No other files were changed for this fix.

nodes.summary: Implemented workspace-aware AI tools for the OpenAI SDK chat flow. The backend now exposes four server-side workspace tools (list artifacts, global search, keyword location, and line-range reads) backed by the Supabase workspace file repository, and the chat route streams tool events plus assistant deltas through SSE. The client now renders tool call cards with stable ids and can jump to artifacts/ranges from tool invocations.

affectedFiles.reason: Added shared workspace file search and read helpers that power all four AI tools without duplicating repository logic.
affectedFiles.notes: Includes listing, global search, keyword location, and line-range extraction helpers.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the OpenAI tool schemas and workspace-bound invocation preparation layer required for model tool calling.
affectedFiles.notes: Validates tool arguments with Zod and returns structured descriptors for UI events.

affectedFiles.reason: Replaced the old unused OpenAI helper with the new tool-aware chat orchestration flow using the OpenAI SDK.
affectedFiles.notes: Handles streamed deltas, tool call loops, and tool result messages.

affectedFiles.reason: Wired the HTTP chat endpoint to the new OpenAI tool orchestration and SSE event stream for tool calls and tool results.
affectedFiles.notes: Still persists user and assistant messages to Supabase.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the client chat hook to render tool call lifecycle events and keep tool statuses synchronized in the conversation thread.
affectedFiles.notes: Now tracks tool call ids from the SSE stream.

affectedFiles.reason: Expanded the tool call type so client state can identify and update individual tool invocations.
affectedFiles.notes: Added artifactId to support document navigation from tool cards.

affectedFiles.reason: Adjusted the live-message rendering so completed tool call cards do not keep showing the streaming loading indicator.
affectedFiles.notes: Only plain assistant placeholders now render the thinking dots.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated tool call navigation to resolve artifacts by id first and then by name, enabling precise file jumps from tool cards.
affectedFiles.notes: Works with read/range tool results.

affectedFiles.reason: Declared zod as a direct dependency because the new tool layer uses Zod schemas at runtime and type validation boundaries.
affectedFiles.notes: Version added matches the lockfile entry already present in the workspace.

affectedFiles.reason: Kept the root dependency manifest aligned with package.json after adding the direct zod dependency.
affectedFiles.notes: Only the root dependency list was updated; the existing zod package entry already existed in the lockfile.

nodes.summary: Implemented the chat thread ordering fix so streamed assistant/tool content now renders top-to-bottom in chronological order. The live assistant stream in useChat now splits into separate assistant segments around tool calls, preventing post-tool deltas from being appended to the pre-tool placeholder bubble. The task card was moved to done after the change was applied.

affectedFiles.reason: Implemented the streaming message segmentation fix so assistant output is split around tool calls and rendered chronologically in the message list.
affectedFiles.notes: Added active assistant message tracking, tool-boundary placeholder removal, new assistant segment creation after tool calls, and safer error fallback handling.

nodes.summary: Fixed a Next.js production build failure caused by outdated workspace repository calls in workspace API routes. The chat and conversations workspace routes now fetch the authenticated user ID before calling findWorkspaceById, matching the repository signature and preserving user scoping. This addressed the TypeScript build error and aligned the route behavior with existing authorization patterns.

affectedFiles.reason: This route caused the reported TypeScript build failure and now enforces authenticated workspace lookup.
affectedFiles.notes: Added user authentication guard and passed userId to findWorkspaceById.

affectedFiles.reason: This route used the same outdated repository call signature and would also fail type checking.
affectedFiles.notes: Added user authentication guard and passed userId to findWorkspaceById.

affectedFiles.reason: The helper signature defined the contract that the routes had to satisfy.
affectedFiles.notes: No code change was needed, only verification of the two-argument signature.

nodes.summary: Updated the workspace page modal flow so Upload files now chains through workspace creation when no workspace exists. The page tracks a pending upload intent, opens the Create Workspace modal when needed, clears the intent on cancel, and automatically opens the Upload modal after a successful workspace creation. The change stayed localized to src/app/page.tsx and reused existing modal components.

affectedFiles.reason: This file owns the workspace page state and modal orchestration, so it was the correct place to add the create-then-upload handoff.
affectedFiles.notes: Added a pending upload flag, a close handler for the create modal, and automatic upload modal opening after workspace creation.

affectedFiles.reason: The existing create workspace modal needed to be reused as-is, so its close and submit behavior was checked before wiring the new flow.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes were required.

affectedFiles.reason: The upload modal behavior was reviewed to ensure it could still open directly after workspace creation without needing component changes.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes were required.

nodes.summary: Implemented CSV support in the workspace file upload flow. The upload validation allowlist, accept attribute, and user-facing labels now include CSV, and CSV is treated as a supported text-like upload without a separate parser. This was kept as a narrow change to the shared workspace-file boundary and the small UI copy surfaces that mention supported formats.

affectedFiles.reason: Expanded the canonical supported file list and accept/mime mapping to include CSV uploads.
affectedFiles.notes: Shared source of truth for both client and server upload validation.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the unsupported-file error message so it matches the new CSV-capable upload list.
affectedFiles.notes: CSV continues to use the existing plain-text extraction path.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated empty-state guidance so users see CSV listed among supported upload types.
affectedFiles.notes: Copy only; no behavioral changes.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the panel subtitle to keep the displayed supported file types accurate.
affectedFiles.notes: Copy only; no behavioral changes.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the displayed format chips so the upload modal reflects CSV support.
affectedFiles.notes: The input already consumed the shared accept string, so no separate behavior change was needed here.

nodes.summary: Implemented CSV-to-table support for workspace uploads. CSV files now flow through a shared CSV parser/serializer at ingest time, are stored as canonical row text, and are mapped to the spreadsheet artifact viewer so they render as rows and columns instead of plain document lines. The existing line-based search and highlight flow remains intact because CSV rows are still persisted as line-oriented content.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides shared CSV parsing and serialization for both server-side ingest and client-side spreadsheet rendering.
affectedFiles.notes: Contains the canonical row parser/serializer used by the new CSV table flow.

affectedFiles.reason: Uses the shared CSV helper to parse CSV uploads into canonical row text before storing extracted lines.
affectedFiles.notes: CSV now gets row-aware preprocessing while other file types keep their existing extraction paths.

affectedFiles.reason: Routes CSV workspace files to the sheet artifact type so the spreadsheet viewer is used.
affectedFiles.notes: Document files still use the text viewer.

affectedFiles.reason: Parses CSV content with the shared helper to render accurate rows and columns instead of splitting by commas naively.
affectedFiles.notes: Keeps the existing line-number/highlight behavior.

nodes.summary: Refined the CSV spreadsheet viewer after initial table support landed. The row-number column now includes a left border so its cell edge matches the rest of the table, while the sticky bottom horizontal scrollbar arrangement remains in place for easier sideways scrolling. This change was limited to the ArtifactViewer spreadsheet component and did not alter CSV parsing or ingest behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the missing left border to the row-number cells and preserved the sticky bottom scrollbar layout.
affectedFiles.notes: This is the only file changed in this follow-up fix.

nodes.summary: Updated the assistant persona and chat empty state to be document-focused rather than code-centric. The persona prompt now describes Canvas as a professional workspace assistant for documents, notes, files, and project work, and the command center empty state now offers document-oriented suggestions instead of lint/architecture prompts.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the assistant identity and behavior guidance to be document-focused rather than code-centric.
affectedFiles.notes: Reworded the assistant description, role, and behavior sections.

affectedFiles.reason: Changed the empty-state copy and suggested actions so the chat UI no longer centers coding tasks like linting or architecture checks.
affectedFiles.notes: Only the empty-state text and suggestion buttons changed.

nodes.summary: Aligned the left sidebar create-workspace plus button with the workspace files header plus button by centralizing it in a shared component that now uses the same compact square shape and theme classes. The collapsed sidebar still hides the button entirely to avoid overlap with the Canvas icon.

affectedFiles.reason: Changed the shared create-workspace button to match the workspace files button shape and styling contract.
affectedFiles.notes: Now requires theme classes via colorClass and uses the compact square icon-button form.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the sidebar header to use the shared create-workspace button with workspace-files styling and to keep it hidden when collapsed.
affectedFiles.notes: Collapsed state remains overlap-free.

affectedFiles.reason: Updated the workspace page header to use the shared create-workspace button with the same styling contract.
affectedFiles.notes: Imports useThemeClasses so the button matches the workspace files button theme classes.

affectedFiles.reason: Continues exporting the shared UI primitive so callers can import the normalized button consistently.
affectedFiles.notes: No API shape change beyond the button component itself.

nodes.summary: Added Docker support for the Next.js app so it can be built and run in a container on port 3075 with restart policy always. The implementation keeps the app entrypoint unchanged and uses a multi-stage image plus docker compose for runtime configuration.

affectedFiles.reason: Defines the production container build and runtime configuration for the Next.js app.
affectedFiles.notes: Multi-stage build; exposes port 3075 and starts Next with explicit host/port flags.

affectedFiles.reason: Provides the service definition with restart policy always and port mapping for local container orchestration.
affectedFiles.notes: Service maps 3075:3075 and sets runtime PORT/HOSTNAME env vars.

affectedFiles.reason: Reduces build context size and prevents local artifacts from being sent to Docker during builds.
affectedFiles.notes: Ignores node_modules, .next, and common log files.

affectedFiles.reason: Documents how to build and run the containerized app on port 3075 with docker compose.
affectedFiles.notes: Added a concise Docker usage section.

nodes.summary: Updated the Next.js config to allow the dev origin testing-canvas.echosphere.cfd while preserving the existing React compiler setting. The project uses a TypeScript next.config.ts with a default export, so the change was made in that style instead of switching to CommonJS.

affectedFiles.reason: Added the allowedDevOrigins entry for the specified development origin while preserving existing Next.js configuration.
affectedFiles.notes: The config remains typed with NextConfig and exported as a default ES module.

nodes.summary: Fixed the login form autocomplete warning by adding autocomplete attributes to the email and password inputs in the login page. The Cloudflare Insights beacon error was identified as a blocked third-party analytics request from the browser environment and not an application bug.

affectedFiles.reason: Added autocomplete attributes to the login form fields to satisfy browser expectations and improve password manager support.
affectedFiles.notes: Email uses autoComplete=\"email\" and password uses autoComplete=\"current-password\".

nodes.summary: Updated Docker Compose so the Canvas container now receives the root .env file at runtime via env_file, while still exposing port 3075 and using restart: always. The README now documents that docker compose loads the project .env into the container, matching the app’s process.env-based config loading.

affectedFiles.reason: Added env_file so the container receives runtime environment variables from the project .env file.
affectedFiles.notes: Preserves port 3075 mapping and restart policy.

affectedFiles.reason: Documented that docker compose loads the root .env file into the container at runtime.
affectedFiles.notes: Helps operators understand where the container gets its env values.

affectedFiles.reason: Confirmed the application depends on process.env values for server-side configuration, which makes runtime env injection necessary.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes were needed in this module.
